threats.title: JWT Admin Forgery via Hardcoded RSA Private Key
threats.scenario: RSA private key hardcoded at lib/insecurity.ts:23 and served at GET /encryptionkeys/jwt.pem. Attacker downloads key and forges admin JWT accepted by express-jwt@0.1.3 without issuer/audience validation.
threats.controls_in_place: RS256 JWT validation present; public key used for verification

steps: Remove hardcoded private key from source
Load from environment variable or vault
Upgrade express-jwt to 8.x with algorithm allowlist

threats.title: SQL Injection in Login Route — Authentication Bypass
threats.scenario: routes/login.ts:34 uses raw SQL string interpolation with req.body.email. Input ' OR 1=1-- bypasses authentication and logs in as admin without credentials.

threats.title: SQL Injection in Search Route — Unauthenticated Database Dump
threats.scenario: routes/search.ts:23 uses req.query.q in raw SQL LIKE query. Unauthenticated UNION injection dumps entire Users table including MD5 hashes.

steps: Use Sequelize Op.like with bound parameters
Never concatenate user input into SQL

threats.title: RCE via eval() in B2B Order Route
threats.scenario: routes/b2bOrder.ts:23 calls vm.runInContext('safeEval(orderLinesData)', sandbox) with user-controlled req.body.orderLinesData. notevil sandbox is bypassable — allows server-side code execution.

steps: Replace with JSON schema validation using Ajv

threats.title: SSTI via eval() in User Profile Route
threats.scenario: routes/userProfile.ts:62 matches #{...} in username and calls eval(). Attacker sets username to #{require('child_process').execSync('id')} to achieve RCE.

steps: Use safe string templating without code execution

threats.title: SSRF via Profile Image URL Upload
threats.scenario: routes/profileImageUrlUpload.ts:24 performs fetch(req.body.imageUrl) with no URL validation. Authenticated attacker reads internal services or AWS IMDS metadata.

steps: Add URL allowlist validating protocol (https only) and blocking internal hostnames

threats.title: Unauthenticated Admin Configuration Endpoints
threats.scenario: GET /rest/admin/application-configuration and /rest/admin/application-version have no auth middleware at server.ts:604-605. Returns full config including OAuth client ID and feature flags.

steps: Add security.isAuthorized() and security.isAdmin() middleware to both routes

threats.title: OAuth CSRF — Missing state Parameter Validation
threats.scenario: OAuth callback at /rest/oauth2/googleCallback does not validate state parameter. Attacker crafts OAuth flow linking victim account to attacker's Google identity.

steps: Generate cryptographic state value, store in session
Validate on callback before code exchange

threats.title: Insufficient Security Event Logging
threats.scenario: Failed logins, SQL injection attempts, admin route access, and IDOR attempts not logged as security events. Only HTTP access log exists. Post-incident investigation impossible.
threats.controls_in_place: HTTP access log via morgan; JSON audit file

steps: Add structured security event logging for auth failures, authz failures, suspicious patterns

threats.title: No Rate Limiting on Login Endpoint
threats.scenario: POST /rest/user/login has no rate limiting. Unlimited login attempts enable brute-force and resource exhaustion.

steps: Add express-rate-limit middleware to login route (10 req/15min)
